paper_title: Comparative Pollen Morphological Analysis and Its Systematic Implications on Three European Oak (Quercus L., Fagaceae) Species and Their Spontaneous Hybrids

methods.description: This method describes the collection and preparation of pollen samples from various oak species and their hybrids for morphological analysis.

steps.description: Collect inflorescences from oak trees, including hybrids, from various locations.
steps.searchable_summary: collect inflorescences from oak trees in multiple locations

steps.description: Verify plant materials by experts and prepare samples for analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: verify and prepare plant materials for analysis

steps.description: Acetolyse pollen samples using Erdtman's method.
steps.searchable_summary: acetolyse pollen samples using Erdtman's method

methods.description: This method involves the measurement and analysis of pollen grains for various quantitative and qualitative features.

steps.description: Measure pollen grains using a light microscope and analyze for quantitative features.
steps.searchable_summary: measure and analyze pollen grains for quantitative features

steps.description: Analyze pollen grains for qualitative features using a scanning electron microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze pollen grains for qualitative features using SEM

methods.description: This method describes the statistical analysis performed on the pollen traits to assess normality, variance, and relationships.

steps.description: Test the normality of the distributions of the studied traits using the Shapiro-Wilk's normality test.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Shapiro-Wilk's normality test on pollen traits

steps.description: Perform multivariate analysis of variance (MANOVA) to assess the effect of taxa on pollen traits.
steps.searchable_summary: perform MANOVA to assess taxa effect on pollen traits

steps.description: Conduct one-way ANOVA to verify the lack of taxon effect on observed traits.
steps.searchable_summary: perform one-way ANOVA on pollen traits

steps.description: Calculate Pearson's correlation coefficients to assess relationships between observed traits.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Pearson's correlation coefficients for pollen traits

steps.description: Use parallel coordinate plot for visualization of species and hybrids.
steps.searchable_summary: create parallel coordinate plot for species and hybrids visualization

steps.description: Apply analysis of canonical variables to present multitrait assessment of similarity of tested genotypes.
steps.searchable_summary: perform canonical variable analysis for genotype similarity

steps.description: Calculate Mahalanobis' distances for taxa and trees to measure polytrait genotypes similarity.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Mahalanobis' distances for genotype similarity
